steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims obedience must be ‘as close to absolute as possible’ and that ‘even minor infractions should not be ignored.’ The question asks which quote best supports this claim. The needed evidence must show why small violations are dangerous — specifically, how they accumulate and ultimately threaten the state.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — power retention: Addresses how rulers hold power, not how minor violations accumulate into larger threats. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Transgression creeps in unperceived and at last ruins the state': Directly shows the mechanism: small infractions go unnoticed, build silently, and eventually cause ruin — exactly why minor violations cannot be tolerated. ✓
  • Choice C — threat-based compliance: Explains why people obey out of fear, not why small infractions are cumulatively dangerous. ✗
  • Choice D — corruption: Discusses corruption generally, not the specific accumulation of minor violations into catastrophic harm. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
